title: Leaf surface acylsugar extraction and LC-MS
author: Yann-Ru Lou
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.xj2fkqe

steps.title: Prepare the Acylsugar Extraction Solution (3:3:2 Acetonitrile:Isopropanol:water, 0
steps.instruction: Prepare the Acylsugar Extraction Solution (3:3:2 Acetonitrile:Isopropanol:water, 0.1% formic acid, 1 µM telmisartan (internal standard)) To prepare 1000 mL Acylsugar Extraction Solution, combine: 375 mL Acetonitrile 375 mL Isopropanol 250 mL ddH 2 O 1136.38 µL Formic Acid (88%) (0.1% final conc.) 1000 µL 1 mM Telmisartan (dissolved in 100% DMSO; final conc. 1 μM) We suggest always making fresh solution. However, in our experience, extraction buffer could be stored short term at 4℃ or -20℃ with no obvious issues. We did not carry out degradation curve and do not claim to know the best storage condition.

steps.title: Dispense 1 ml of Acylsugar Extraction...
steps.instruction: Dispense 1 ml of Acylsugar Extraction Solution into the labeled extraction vial

steps.title: Using a fine scissors or forceps,...
steps.instruction: Using a fine scissors or forceps, cut 1-2 small new leaflets and immediately place them in the designated extraction vial Clean the forceps and/or scissors with Acetonitrile and a Kimwipe between samples.

steps.title: Tighten the cap well and gently...
steps.instruction: Tighten the cap well and gently agitate the tube on a rocker for 00:02:00

steps.title: Remove the vial from the rocker...
steps.instruction: Remove the vial from the rocker and immediately pipette the extraction solution into the corresponding LC-MS glass vial and seal it with the cap. Make sure the leaflet remains in the extraction vial. Acylsugars are not green, however, disturbance of chlorophyll and/or other metabolites might make the extraction solution turn green-ish looking. Based on our experience, this does not affect the extraction and qualification of acylsugars.

steps.title: Store the LC-MS vials at -20 °C. For quantification purpose
steps.instruction: Store the LC-MS vials at -20 °C. For quantification purpose: Place uncapped extraction vials (containing leaf tissue) in a drying cabinet (~40°C) and discard the extraction vial cap. Weigh dried leaf tissue after 3 days or after thoroughly dried

steps.title: Add 630
steps.instruction: Add 630.6 mg of Ammonium Formate to 950 mL of water Adjust the pH of the solution to 2.8 using formic acid Add water up to 1000 mL Table

steps.title: 21-min LC method %s %s If...
steps.instruction: 21-min LC method %s %s If you are not familiar with LC-MS, we highly recommend getting on-site help from an experienced person.

steps.title: To analyze extraction on LC-MS, we...
steps.instruction: To analyze extraction on LC-MS, we use the following MS settings: For negative ion-mode electrospray ionization: Capillary voltage: 2.00 kV Source temperature: 100°C Desolvation temperature: 350°C Desolvation nitrogen gas flow rate: 600 liters/h Cone voltage: 35V Mass range: m/z 50 to 1000 with spectra accumulated at 0.1 seconds/function Collision energies: Function 1: 0V Function 2: 15V Function 3: 35V For positive ion-mode electrospray ionization: Capillary voltage: 3.00 kV Source temperature: 100°C Desolvation temperature: 350°C Desolvation nitrogen gas flow rate: 600 liters/h Cone voltage: 35V Mass range: m/z 50 to 1000 with spectra accumulated at 0.1 seconds/function Collision energies: Function 1: 0V Function 2: 15V Function 3: 45V Fore more detail, please refer to the experiment files: ESI- experiment file.txt ESI+ experiment file.txt If you are not familiar with LC-MS, we highly recommend getting on-site help from an experienced person.

steps.title: For a list of manuscripts with...
steps.instruction: For a list of manuscripts with useful information regarding acylsugar identification (masses, fragmentation patterns and quanitifcation methods), please refer to the description page of this protocol.
